task:
OBJECTIVE: Create /Users/srini/Projects/kommit/app/commit/page.tsx

Commitment page. User sets their stake amount, chooses where their money goes if they fail, and pays via Stripe.

Add "use client" at top.

Imports needed:
import { useState, useEffect } from "react";
import { loadStripe } from "@stripe/stripe-js";
import { Elements, PaymentElement, useStripe, useElements } from "@stripe/react-stripe-js";

const stripePromise = loadStripe(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY!);

This page reads card data from URL search params: truth, commitment, stake (optional).

State in outer component:
- clientSecret: string | null
- stakeAmount: number (default 500, in cents = $5.00)
- forfeitDestination: string (default "A cause I oppose")
- truth: string (from searchParams)
- commitment: string (from searchParams)

On mount: call POST /api/commitments/create with { stakeAmount, forfeitDestination, commitmentText: commitment } to get clientSecret. Set clientSecret.

Render outer component:
- Show the truth from the card (large, prominent)
- Show the commitment text
- Slider or input for stake amount: minimum $5, maximum $200, step $5. Show the selected amount clearly.
- Text input for forfeit destination: "If I don't follow through, my money goes to:" 
- When clientSecret is ready: render Elements provider with stripe={stripePromise} options={{ clientSecret }}. Inside render CheckoutForm component.

CheckoutForm component:
- Uses useStripe() and useElements()
- On submit: call stripe.confirmPayment({ elements, confirmParams: { return_url: window.location.origin + "/success" } })
- Submit button: "Back this with $" + formatted stake amount
- Show error if payment fails.

No Tailwind. Background #FAFAF8, accent #3D5A36.

FILES: /Users/srini/Projects/kommit/app/commit/page.tsx

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
"use client" at top
Reads truth and commitment from searchParams
Renders Stripe Elements when clientSecret available
No TypeScript errors

TEST: cd /Users/srini/Projects/kommit; npx tsc --noEmit

DO NOT: use Tailwind
DO NOT: use ast.parse
